Cloud accounting software for invoicing,: Cloud accounting software for invoicing, bills, expenses, and tax reporting (ZATCA, UAE FTA).
\n**How to get your Wafeq API Key:**\n\n:
**How to get your Wafeq API Key:**

1. Log in to your [Wafeq account](https://app.wafeq.com/accounts/signup/).
2. In the left-side menu, open the **Developer** section and click **API Keys**.
3. Click **Create new key** and copy the key — you won't be able to see it again.
4. Click **Enable API**. The API is available on the **Starter plan** or higher (including free trials).
5. Paste the key below.
Create a new sales invoice for a custome: Create a new sales invoice for a customer.
Submit an invoice to the tax authority (: Submit an invoice to the tax authority (ZATCA in Saudi Arabia, FTA in UAE). The invoice must already be finalized in Wafeq before you can report it.
Add a new customer or supplier to your W: Add a new customer or supplier to your Wafeq address book so you can invoice them or record bills from them.
Search your Wafeq contacts by name, emai: Search your Wafeq contacts by name, email, phone, or tax number. Useful for checking if a customer already exists before creating a new one.
Record a bill you received from a suppli: Record a bill you received from a supplier (e.g. a vendor invoice you need to pay).
Record money received from a customer (p: Record money received from a customer (paying an invoice) or money sent to a supplier (paying a bill).
Create a simplified (retail) tax invoice: Create a simplified (retail) tax invoice — used for walk-in / over-the-counter sales, especially in Saudi Arabia under ZATCA. Use a regular invoice for B2B sales instead.
Issue a credit note to a customer — used: Issue a credit note to a customer — used when you refund them, correct an overcharge, or take back goods. A credit note reduces how much the customer owes you.
Send a customer a quote (also called an: Send a customer a quote (also called an estimate). Quotes can later be converted into an invoice with the "Convert Quote to Invoice" action.
Turn a customer-accepted quote into a re: Turn a customer-accepted quote into a real invoice. The new invoice copies the customer, line items, and totals from the quote.
Download an invoice as a PDF. You can at: Download an invoice as a PDF. You can attach the file to a Gmail / Outlook email, save it to Google Drive or Dropbox, or upload it anywhere else in the flow.
Add a product or service to your Wafeq c: Add a product or service to your Wafeq catalog. Catalog items can be quickly added to invoices, bills, and quotes without retyping descriptions or prices.
List products and services from your Waf: List products and services from your Wafeq catalog. Useful for syncing your catalog to a spreadsheet or another system.
List accounts from your Wafeq chart of a: List accounts from your Wafeq chart of accounts. Useful for exporting your chart of accounts or finding account IDs you need elsewhere.
Make a custom API call to a specific end: 将一个自定义 API 调用到一个特定的终点
Do the prices above include tax?: Do the prices above include tax?
Name: 名称
Who are you billing? Pick from your Wafe: Who are you billing? Pick from your Wafeq contacts.
A unique number for this invoice (e.g. I: A unique number for this invoice (e.g. INV-2024-001). If you reuse a number, Wafeq will reject the invoice.
The date shown on the invoice. Usually t: The date shown on the invoice. Usually today.
The date you expect to be paid by.
Which currency the amounts below are in. Pick AED for UAE, SAR for Saudi Arabia, or any other currency you invoice in.
Where the money from this invoice is boo: Where the money from this invoice is booked (e.g. "Sales Revenue"). This is applied to every line item unless you override it on a specific line.
Tax applied to every line by default (e.g. "VAT 15%"). Leave blank if this invoice has no tax.
Add one row for each product or service: Add one row for each product or service on the document. Each row needs at least a description, quantity, and unit price. Leave the account and tax fields blank to use the defaults you set above.
If you already baked tax into the unit p: If you already baked tax into the unit prices, pick "Yes". If tax should be added on top of the prices, pick "No". Most B2B invoices use "No".
Draft = still editable, not posted to yo: Draft = still editable, not posted to your books yet. Sent = emailed to the customer. Finalized = locked and posted to your books. Start with Draft if unsure.
Language used on the PDF invoice sent to: Language used on the PDF invoice sent to the customer.
Optional text printed on the invoice (e.g. a project name or the customer's PO number).
Optional notes printed at the bottom of: Optional notes printed at the bottom of the invoice (e.g. payment instructions, thank-you note).
Optional. Store an ID from another syste: Optional. Store an ID from another system here (e.g. the order number from your online store) so you can find this record in Wafeq later.
Advanced. If the flow retries this step,: Advanced. If the flow retries this step, sending the same key prevents creating a duplicate. Most users can leave this blank.
Pick which invoice to submit. ⚠️ The inv: Pick which invoice to submit. ⚠️ The invoice must be in "Finalized" status first — if you just created it as a Draft, change its status to Finalized in Wafeq before running this action.
The business name (for companies) or ful: The business name (for companies) or full name (for individuals). This is what appears on invoices and bills.
Pick \"Customer\" if you sell to them, \: Pick "Customer" if you sell to them, "Supplier" if you buy from them, or both if they're on both sides.
Where to send invoices and other documen: Where to send invoices and other documents. Optional but recommended — Wafeq can email invoices directly.
The contact's VAT registration number (a: The contact's VAT registration number (also called TRN in UAE, TIN in some countries). Required for tax-compliant B2B invoices — leave blank for individuals.
Two-letter ISO country code: AE = UAE, S: Two-letter ISO country code: AE = UAE, SA = Saudi Arabia, US = United States, GB = UK, etc.
Street / building address. This appears: Street / building address. This appears on printed invoices.
The postal code. Must be exactly 5 digit: The postal code. Must be exactly 5 digits for Saudi Arabia addresses.
Type what you're looking for — part of a: Type what you're looking for — part of a name, email, phone number, or tax number works. Leave blank to list all contacts.
Only show customers, only suppliers, or: Only show customers, only suppliers, or both.
If you stored a reference ID when creati: If you stored a reference ID when creating the contact (e.g. your CRM's customer ID), enter it here for an exact match.
How many contacts to return at most. Def: How many contacts to return at most. Default is 50, maximum is 200.
Who sent you this bill? Pick from your W: Who sent you this bill? Pick from your Wafeq contacts.
The invoice number printed on the suppli: The invoice number printed on the supplier's bill (so you can match it later). Must be unique per supplier in your Wafeq org.
The date printed on the supplier's bill.
The date you need to pay this bill by.
Where this cost is booked in your books: Where this cost is booked in your books (e.g. "Office Supplies", "Software Subscriptions"). Applied to every line item unless you override it on a specific line.
Tax applied to every line by default (e.g. "VAT 15% on purchases"). Leave blank if no tax.
If the supplier's prices already include: If the supplier's prices already include tax, pick "Yes". If tax should be added on top, pick "No".
Draft = still editable, not in your book: Draft = still editable, not in your books yet. Authorized = approved and posted to your books. Start with Draft if unsure.
Optional free text (e.g. project name, d: Optional free text (e.g. project name, department).
Optional. The purchase order number from: Optional. The purchase order number from your own system, if you issued one.
Optional notes visible in Wafeq. Not pri: Optional notes visible in Wafeq. Not printed on anything and not shared with the supplier.
Pick what the money was used to pay. \"A: Pick what the money was used to pay. "Advanced" lets you split a single payment across multiple invoices or bills.
The full amount of the payment, includin: The full amount of the payment, including any fees. For a simple single-document payment, this should match the unpaid balance.
The amount in the payment currency. Must: The amount in the payment currency. Must be greater than zero.
When the payment was made or received. U: When the payment was made or received. Use the date on your bank statement.
Which bank, cash, or card account the mo: Which bank, cash, or card account the money moved through. Only payment-enabled accounts are shown.
Optional. The customer or supplier this: Optional. The customer or supplier this payment is with. Usually auto-detected from the document.
Optional. Any bank or card processing fe: Optional. Any bank or card processing fees deducted from the payment (e.g. Stripe fee). Leave blank if none.
Only needed if you entered Payment Fees: Only needed if you entered Payment Fees above. Pick the expense account the fees are booked to (e.g. "Bank Charges").
Optional. A bank transaction ID or any n: Optional. A bank transaction ID or any note you want to attach.
The date the sale happened. Usually toda: The date the sale happened. Usually today.
Pick the bank, cash drawer, or POS accou: Pick the bank, cash drawer, or POS account the customer paid into. Only payment-enabled accounts are shown.
Where the sale is booked (e.g. \"Sales R: Where the sale is booked (e.g. "Sales Revenue"). Applied to every line unless overridden.
Tax applied to every line by default (e.g. "VAT 15%").
Retail prices almost always include tax,: Retail prices almost always include tax, so "Yes" is recommended.
Optional. Most simplified invoices are f: Optional. Most simplified invoices are for walk-in customers and leave this blank.
Leave blank to let Wafeq auto-generate o: Leave blank to let Wafeq auto-generate one.
Paid = the sale is complete. Draft = not: Paid = the sale is complete. Draft = not posted to books yet.
Optional free text shown on the invoice.
Optional notes printed on the invoice.
Which customer is the credit note for?: Which customer is the credit note for?
A unique number for this credit note (e.g. CN-2024-001).
The date shown on the credit note. Usual: The date shown on the credit note. Usually today.
The sales account the credit reverses ag: The sales account the credit reverses against (usually the same account the original invoice posted to). Applied to every line unless overridden per line.
Tax applied to every line by default. Le: Tax applied to every line by default. Leave blank for no tax.
Draft = still editable. Sent = marked as: Draft = still editable. Sent = marked as sent to the customer. Finalized = locked and posted to your books.
Optional text shown on the credit note (: Optional text shown on the credit note (e.g. the original invoice number this refunds).
Optional notes printed on the credit not: Optional notes printed on the credit note (e.g. reason for the credit).
A unique number for this quote (e.g. QT-: A unique number for this quote (e.g. QT-2024-001). Must not reuse an existing quote number.
The date shown on the quote. Usually tod: The date shown on the quote. Usually today.
Where the revenue is booked when this qu: Where the revenue is booked when this quote becomes an invoice. Applied to every line unless overridden per line.
Pick \"Yes\" if the prices already inclu: Pick "Yes" if the prices already include tax, "No" if tax should be added on top.
Draft = still editable. Sent = marked as: Draft = still editable. Sent = marked as sent to the customer. Start with Draft if unsure.
Language used on the quote PDF.
Optional. If the customer sent you a pur: Optional. If the customer sent you a purchase order number, enter it here so it appears on the quote.
Optional free text shown on the quote (e: Optional free text shown on the quote (e.g. project name).
Optional notes printed at the bottom of: Optional notes printed at the bottom of the quote.
Pick the quote you want to convert into: Pick the quote you want to convert into an invoice.
What to call the downloaded PDF. Leave b: What to call the downloaded PDF. Leave blank to use "invoice-<id>.pdf". The ".pdf" extension is added automatically if you don't include it.
What the product or service is called (e: What the product or service is called (e.g. "Pro subscription", "Consulting hour"). This shows up in item pickers.
Optional longer description used as the: Optional longer description used as the default line text when this item is added to an invoice.
Optional short code (e.g. \"SUB-PRO-YR\": Optional short code (e.g. "SUB-PRO-YR"). Useful if you also track the item in another system.
How much you charge customers per unit. Used as the default on invoices and quotes.
How much you pay suppliers per unit. Use: How much you pay suppliers per unit. Used as the default on bills.
Advanced. The account ID sales of this i: Advanced. The account ID sales of this item post to (e.g. "Sales Revenue"). Leave blank to use your org default.
Advanced. The account ID purchases of th: Advanced. The account ID purchases of this item post to.
Advanced. Tax rate ID applied when selli: Advanced. Tax rate ID applied when selling this item. Leave blank for no default tax.
Advanced. Tax rate ID applied when buyin: Advanced. Tax rate ID applied when buying this item.
Turn on for physical products where you: Turn on for physical products where you want Wafeq to track inventory counts. Leave off for services and digital products.
Active items can be added to new invoice: Active items can be added to new invoices. Turn off to hide the item from pickers without deleting it.
Find a single item by the reference ID y: Find a single item by the reference ID you stored when creating it (e.g. your Shopify product ID).
Only return items created on or after th: Only return items created on or after this date.
Only return items created on or before t: Only return items created on or before this date.
How many items to return at most. Defaul: How many items to return at most. Default is 50, maximum is 200.
Filter to one kind of account. Revenue =: Filter to one kind of account. Revenue = sales accounts, Expense = cost accounts, Asset = bank / inventory, Liability = loans / payables, Equity = owner's capital.
Payment-enabled accounts are the bank, c: Payment-enabled accounts are the bank, cash, or card accounts you can receive or send money from.
Return the single account matching this: Return the single account matching this reference ID (if you've stored one).
How many accounts to return at most. Def: How many accounts to return at most. Default is 100, maximum is 500.
Authorization headers are injected autom: 授权头自动从您的连接中注入。
Enable for files like PDFs, images, etc.
No — add tax on top of the prices: No — add tax on top of the prices
Yes — prices already include tax (recomm: Yes — prices already include tax (recommended for retail)
HEAD: 黑色
Fires every time a new invoice is create: Fires every time a new invoice is created in Wafeq. Useful for logging sales to a spreadsheet, posting to Slack, or syncing to another system.
Fires when a new bill from a supplier is: Fires when a new bill from a supplier is created. Useful for approval workflows — e.g. post the new bill to a Slack channel for a manager to authorize.
Fires every time a new customer or suppl: Fires every time a new customer or supplier is added to your Wafeq address book. Useful for syncing new customers to HubSpot, Mailchimp, or your CRM.
Fires every time a payment is recorded i: Fires every time a payment is recorded in Wafeq. Great for celebrating revenue in Slack, triggering commission calculations, or sending the customer a receipt.
Only fire on new customers, only supplie: Only fire on new customers, only suppliers, or both.
